title: Ensure In-transit Encryption is enabled on Compute Instance

input_row.title: Ensure In-transit Encryption is enabled on Compute Instance
input_row.description: The Block Volume service provides the option to enable in-transit encryption for paravirtualized volume attachments on virtual machine (VM) instances.
input_row.rationale: All the data moving between the instance and the block volume is transferred over an internal and highly secure network. If you have specific compliance requirements related to the encryption of the data while it is moving between the instance and the block volume, you should enable the in-transit encryption option.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Go to https://cloud.oracle.com/compute/instances 2. Select compute instance in your compartment. 3. Click on each instance name. 4. Click on Boot volume on the bottom left. 5. Under the In-transit encryption column make sure it is Enabled From CLI: 1. Execute the following: for region in `oci iam region-subscription list | jq -r '.data[] | ."region- name"'`; do for compid in `oci iam compartment list --compartment-id-in-subtree TRUE 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.data[] | .id'` do output=`oci compute instance list --compartment-id $compid -- region $region --all 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.data[] | select(."launch- options"."is-pv-encryption-in-transit-enabled" == false )'` if [ ! -z "$output" ]; then echo $output; fi done done 2. Ensure no results are returned
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Navigate to https://cloud.oracle.com/compute/instances 2. Select the instance from the Audit Procedure 3. Click Terminate. 4. Determine whether or not to permanently delete instance's attached boot volume. 5. Click Terminate instance. 6. Click on Create Instance. 7. Fill in the details as per requirements. 8. In the Boot volume section ensure Use in-transit encryption is checked. 9. Fill in the rest of the details as per requirements. 10. Click Create.
input_row.impact: In-transit encryption for boot and block volumes is only available for virtual machine (VM) instances launched from platform images, along with bare metal instances that use the following shapes: BM.Standard.E3.128, BM.Standard.E4.128, BM.DenseIO.E4.128. It is not supported on other bare metal instances.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit indicates steps that can be executed via OCI CLI, and the JSON structure provides clear API-queryable fields. The description mentions in-transit encryption for block volumes associated with compute instances, which is a standard cloud service property.
- The OCI Python SDK and CLI commands are referenced in the audit section, affirming the property can be queried programmatically.
- No manual steps or human judgment are required for this control as the check is specific to configuration settings accessible through APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** oci_compute_instance_in_transit_encryption_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the following OCI CLI commands to check for compliance:
  1. **List all compute instances across all compartments and regions**:
     ```bash
     for region in `oci iam region-subscription list | jq -r '.data[] | .\"region-name\"'`; do
         for compid in `oci iam compartment list --compartment-id-in-subtree TRUE 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.data[] | .id'`; do
             oci compute instance list --compartment-id $compid --region $region --all --query "data[?launchOptions.isPvEncryptionInTransitEnabled==\`false\`]"
         done
     done
     ```

- **Specific configuration properties/settings to validate**: 
  - Check the `launchOptions.isPvEncryptionInTransitEnabled` attribute, which should return `true` for compliant instances.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria**:
  - Pass: No results returned from the above command (all instances have in-transit encryption enabled).
  - Fail: Any instance returns as `false` for the `launchOptions.isPvEncryptionInTransitEnabled` attribute, indicating in-transit encryption is not enabled.

This approach ensures automated assessment of compliance regarding in-transit encryption for compute instances in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.
